title: goodyera rostrata
intro: Goodyera rostrata, also known as the ruffled rattlesnake plantain, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds due to its antitussive and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Goodyera rostrata include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and promoting easier breathing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the roots or rhizomes in hot water to create a tea, which can be used to treat respiratory ailments.
